spec.title: Engine-port runner crate template and ABI conformance

humanSummary.text:
UTSUSHI-103 Slice A delivers the engine-neutral substrate as planned. The new `utsushi_core::port` module (`manifest`, `trait_`, `runner`, `diagnostics`, `conformance`, `mod`) is engine-neutral: a grep for xp3/kirikiri/kag/rgss/rpgmaker/reallive/siglus across the module returns only one match, a doc-comment example in manifest.rs. `EnginePort` declares `const MANIFEST: PortManifest` (the const associated value justifies the generic `Runner

` choice the plan documents in section 10.4); the four required lifecycle methods (launch, observe, capture, shutdown) have no default impl, so omission is a compile error. `jump` defaults to `Err(CapabilityUnsupported { reason: DefaultUnimplemented })`. `PortManifest::validate` enforces id shape, semver triple, required-method set equality with `REQUIRED_LIFECYCLE_STAGES`, optional-method membership in `OPTIONAL_LIFECYCLE_STAGES`, capability/lifecycle-method coherence (drift caught as `ManifestCapabilityDrift::UnclaimedImplementation`), tier ceiling, env-key shape, env-field deduplication, and forbidden env shapes — `EnvFieldShape::{Path, LocalPath, Secret}` rejected at construct-time via `EnvFieldSchema::validate` (test `validate_rejects_env_schema_with_*_shape`), with `EnvFieldSchema::validate_value` additionally filtering runtime values through `looks_like_local_path`. `Runner::validate_manifest` layers the ABI membership check (`SUPPORTED_ABI_VERSIONS = &[1]`) and is called from every `run_*` entry. `run_lifecycle` enforces env values, manages cancellation, drains observations under a 4096-event cap, re-validates each `ObservationHookEvent`, and always invokes `shutdown` after the primary flow (primary error promoted, then shutdown error). `ensure_capture_within_root` rejects an out-of-root artifact path with `ArtifactRootViolation`. `RunnerCancellation` is cooperative (`Arc` SeqCst). `RuntimeRequest` is extended additively (`cancellation`, `replay_log`, `sinks`), with `Debug` redacted via static labels (test `runtime_request_debug_does_not_leak_cancellation_or_replay_log`). The forward-additive trait stubs (`ReplayLogHandle`, `RuntimeSinks`) are empty, `#[doc(hidden)]`, and tagged with owning-node TODOs as promised. `looks_like_local_path` was widened crate-private -> `pub`; the diff is one line plus an audit-grade doc comment, with no signature change, satisfying the minimal-widening bar. `EnginePortAdapter

` correctly hides the generic behind a `Mutex

` and a pre-validated descriptor; `discover_branches` correctly falls back to the legacy `RuntimeAdapter` default. utsushi-fixture has zero diff vs main (`git diff main..HEAD -- crates/utsushi-fixture/` is empty), satisfying the explicit Slice B deferral. `launch_adapters.rs` correctly remains unmigrated per the plan. Verification reruns from the worktree match the worker's report: `cargo test -p utsushi-core engine_port` passes 3 integration tests plus 1 unit test (other test files are filtered out by the binary filter), `cargo test -p utsushi-core` passes 117 (84 lib + 17 engine_port unit + 16 vfs_synthetic_package), `cargo test -p utsushi-fixture` passes 22, `cargo clippy -p utsushi-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean, `cargo fmt --check` clean, `just check` green (524 nodes, schema gates, typecheck, workspace check). Acceptance criteria: (1) the conformance harness exists and the synthetic `ReferencePort` passes the full positive lifecycle (launch + 1 observation + capture under managed root + idempotent shutdown + cancellation observance + Jump-not-declared diagnostic), satisfying the Slice A clarification; (2) jump-unsupported produces typed `CapabilityUnsupported` (runner gate uses `NotYetSupported`, default trait impl uses `DefaultUnimplemented`); (3) missing required methods, unsupported ABI versions, and unredacted env values all surface as typed diagnostics through the harness; (4) the runner template lives in engine-neutral `utsushi-core::port` with no engine-specific code. Both worker deviations are acceptable: invoking `Jump` directly to catch unclaimed implementations is the only way to detect drift on a capability that the runner gate would otherwise short-circuit (an alternative architectural choice would change the trait surface; not a Slice A blocker), and reserving `DefaultUnimplemented` for the trait default path while runner-gated rejections use `NotYetSupported` is a defensible split that the integration test acknowledges by matching either variant. No P0/P1 findings. Three P2 follow-ups recorded as DAG nodes for Slice B fixture adoption, managed `RuntimeArtifactRoot` propagation through `RuntimeRequest`, and a typed diagnostic for `EnginePortAdapter`'s `BranchDiscovery` rejection (currently stringly-typed via `format!(...).into()`).

findings.title: Slice B fixture adoption: refactor FixtureRuntimeAdapter through EnginePortAdapter<FixtureEnginePort>
findings.description: Slice A intentionally leaves utsushi-fixture unchanged (git diff main..HEAD -- crates/utsushi-fixture/ is empty). The plan's acceptance criterion 1 reads in two phases: the conformance harness exists and a reference port passes (Slice A), and the fixture-adapter refactor lands as the reference non-synthetic consumer (Slice B). Until the fixture refactor lands, the conformance harness has only synthetic ports as evidence; the wider claim that 'the fixture port passes ABI conformance for launch, observation envelope emission, cancellation, artifact-root usage, and shutdown' has not been demonstrated against the real fixture port.

evidence.detail: git -C /scratch/worktrees/itotori-spec-utsushi-103 diff main..HEAD -- crates/utsushi-fixture/ returned an empty diff.
Plan section 12 explicitly scopes the fixture refactor as Slice B (UTSUSHI-103b-fixture-adoption).
cargo test -p utsushi-fixture continues to pass (22/22) with no behavior change because the fixture has not adopted the template.

findings.impact: Slice A is mergeable as substrate, but the engine-neutral conformance claim is unproven against a real engine port until Slice B lands. Engine-port crates downstream (UTSUSHI-146 utsushi-reallive, UTSUSHI-031..039) need the fixture adoption as their reference example.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A new FixtureEnginePort struct implements EnginePort with a const MANIFEST declaring [Launch, Observe, Capture, Shutdown] capabilities, abi_version 1, fidelity_tier_max LayoutProbe, evidence_tier_max E2, and no env_schema.
FixtureRuntimeAdapter becomes EnginePortAdapter<FixtureEnginePort> (or an alias) so utsushi-cli continues to compile unchanged.

crates/utsushi-fixture/tests/abi_conformance.rs runs run_required_abi against the fixture port and asserts launched + observation_count >= 1 + captured + first_shutdown_clean + second_shutdown_idempotent + cancellation_observed + JumpOutcome::NotDeclared.
Existing fixture descriptor round-trips through the manifest (id, version, fidelity tier, evidence ceiling).

proposedDagNode.title: Fixture engine port adoption (UTSUSHI-103 Slice B)
proposedDagNode.summary: Refactor utsushi-fixture so FixtureRuntimeAdapter is built on the UTSUSHI-103 EnginePort substrate, providing the first non-synthetic conformance proof and the canonical reference port that downstream engine crates (UTSUSHI-146, UTSUSHI-031..039) imitate.
deliverables: FixtureEnginePort struct implementing EnginePort with a const MANIFEST
FixtureRuntimeAdapter as EnginePortAdapter<FixtureEnginePort> (alias or thin newtype)
Round-trip test confirming descriptor fields derive from the manifest
acceptanceCriteria: FixtureEnginePort passes the ABI conformance harness for launch, observation envelope emission, cancellation, artifact-root usage, and shutdown idempotence.
utsushi-cli runtime registry compiles and runs unchanged.
Existing fixture trace, capture, and smoke_validate goldens continue to pass.
auditFocus: Behavior parity with the pre-Slice-B fixture adapter
Conformance harness coverage of the real (non-synthetic) port
Descriptor drift between manifest and RuntimeAdapterDescriptor

findings.title: EnginePortAdapter shim does not hydrate PortRequest::artifact_root from legacy RuntimeRequest::artifact_root
findings.description: In `EnginePortAdapter::run_lifecycle`, the inline comment (lines 93-98) acknowledges that the Slice A shim does not hydrate `port_request.artifact_root` from the legacy `RuntimeRequest::artifact_root` because the former takes `&RuntimeArtifactRoot` while the latter is `Option<&Path>`. As a result, any engine port driven through the legacy `RuntimeAdapter` surface receives `artifact_root = None` and either falls back to its own ad-hoc handling or fails to satisfy the runner's `ensure_capture_within_root` check (which is no-op when `request.artifact_root` is None — see runner.rs line 202). The integration test `synthetic_port_capture_writes_into_managed_artifact_root` only exercises the direct `Runner::run_capture` path with a hand-built `PortRequest::with_artifact_root`, not the adapter bridge. A typed managed-root carrier on `RuntimeRequest` lets the bridge stop being a Slice A wart and lets the runner's containment check fire on every adapter call.

evidence.detail: Inline acknowledgement of the gap and the reason (RuntimeArtifactRoot vs &Path mismatch).
ensure_capture_within_root is conditional on request.artifact_root being Some.

engine_port_adapter_trace_runs_lifecycle_and_returns_runtime_evidence_report_v02 exercises Trace via the bridge, but the capture path of EnginePortAdapter is not covered by a managed-root containment assertion.

findings.impact: Engine ports driven through `EnginePortAdapter` lose the runner's artifact-root containment guarantee until Slice B (or this work) lands. ports may write outside the managed root and the runner cannot detect it. Once UTSUSHI-031..039 engine ports start consuming the adapter, this gap silently weakens the cross-port artifact-isolation guarantee.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: RuntimeRequest exposes a typed managed artifact root carrier (either `Option<&RuntimeArtifactRoot>` or an equivalent type-erased handle) so EnginePortAdapter can hydrate PortRequest::artifact_root without the &Path mismatch.

EnginePortAdapter::run_lifecycle hydrates PortRequest::artifact_root from the new field, the inline Slice A follow-up comment is removed, and the runner's containment check fires on every adapter-driven capture.
Existing callers continue to compile (additive widening; remove the Slice A note from mod.rs when done).
A new test asserts that EnginePortAdapter::capture rejects an out-of-root artifact URI with ArtifactRootViolation.

proposedDagNode.title: Managed RuntimeArtifactRoot on RuntimeRequest for engine-port bridge
proposedDagNode.summary: Widen RuntimeRequest to carry the managed RuntimeArtifactRoot (not just an Option<&Path>) so EnginePortAdapter can hydrate PortRequest::artifact_root and the runner's artifact-root containment check fires on every legacy-adapter-driven capture.
deliverables: Additive RuntimeRequest field that carries Option<&RuntimeArtifactRoot> (or an equivalent typed handle)
EnginePortAdapter::run_lifecycle hydrates PortRequest::artifact_root from the new field
Slice A inline TODO comment removed from crates/utsushi-core/src/port/mod.rs
New test: EnginePortAdapter::capture rejects an out-of-root artifact URI with ArtifactRootViolation
acceptanceCriteria: EnginePortAdapter-driven capture passes the runner's ensure_capture_within_root check when the legacy RuntimeRequest supplies the managed root.
Existing callers that supply only Option<&Path> continue to compile.
Adapter-driven capture writing outside the managed root is rejected with EnginePortError::ArtifactRootViolation.
auditFocus: Artifact-root containment via the legacy adapter surface
Additive RuntimeRequest evolution under concurrent specs
Behavioural parity between direct Runner calls and EnginePortAdapter calls

findings.title: EnginePortAdapter::discover_branches rejection is stringly-typed
findings.description: When `EnginePortAdapter::run_lifecycle` is asked to fulfil `RuntimeOperation::BranchDiscovery`, it returns `format!("engine port adapter {} does not support {}", ...).into()`. This is a stringly-typed `Box<dyn std::error::Error>` rather than a typed `CapabilityUnsupported { capability: ..., reason: NotYetSupported }` or the existing `unsupported_operation` helper used by `RuntimeAdapter`'s default `discover_branches` (lib.rs line 3597). Two consequences: (a) downstream consumers cannot match on a structured rejection to decide whether to retry against a different adapter, and (b) the diagnostic format diverges from the rest of the engine-port surface, breaking the auditFocus[1] 'ABI drift between ports' guarantee that every adapter that adopts the template can be inspected through one shape. Note: `RuntimeAdapter::discover_branches` is intentionally outside the EnginePort surface (plan section 10.5), so the right fix is a typed adapter-level diagnostic, not adding a method to the trait.

evidence.detail: The legacy RuntimeAdapter default uses the typed unsupported_operation helper.

findings.impact: Mid-priority: the rejection still fails the call, so it is not a correctness gap. But it leaks a string-formatted diagnostic into the runtime registry instead of a typed one, weakening the orchestration auditFocus[1] claim and forcing downstream code that wants to react to 'this adapter does not support branch discovery' to substring-match.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: EnginePortAdapter::run_lifecycle's BranchDiscovery arm returns a typed rejection — either reuse the existing unsupported_operation helper to produce a RuntimeAdapterDiagnostic, or extend EnginePortError with an `AdapterOperationUnsupported { operation: RuntimeOperation }` variant and map it through the existing `Box<dyn std::error::Error>` channel.
The chosen variant is round-trippable: downstream code can match on the typed rejection rather than substring-matching the Display output.
A regression test asserts that calling discover_branches via an EnginePortAdapter returns the typed rejection and that its Display passes looks_like_local_path.

proposedDagNode.title: Typed diagnostic for EnginePortAdapter branch-discovery rejection
proposedDagNode.summary: Replace EnginePortAdapter::run_lifecycle's stringly-typed BranchDiscovery rejection with either the existing unsupported_operation helper or a typed AdapterOperationUnsupported variant on EnginePortError so downstream code can match structurally.
deliverables: Typed rejection path for BranchDiscovery in EnginePortAdapter::run_lifecycle
Regression test verifying the typed shape and redacted Display
acceptanceCriteria: EnginePortAdapter-driven discover_branches returns a typed rejection that downstream code can match without substring-parsing.
Display of the rejection passes looks_like_local_path.
Existing engine_port and utsushi-fixture tests continue to pass.
auditFocus: ABI drift between ports (typed vs stringly-typed rejections)
Downstream registry behavior when an operation is unsupported
